note: 2026-05-30 full window (56 completed runs, 91.1% success). 5 failures across 5 DISTINCT classes. Yesterday's critical cache-memory git bug did NOT recur. target=* reactivated; token-budget 429 recurred.

issues.title: Token-budget 429: Linter Miner exceeded 25M effective-token cap (25.13M/25M), all retries exhausted -> run failed
issues.detail: Run 26690626184 (Linter Miner, copilot engine, claude-sonnet-4.6, CLI 1.0.55). After 59 turns / 2.44M raw tokens the copilot harness hit 'CAPIError: 429 Maximum effective tokens exceeded (25132364.10 / 25000000)'. It retried 4 attempts with --continue (isCAPIError400=false, not auth/model errors), each re-hitting the 429, then 'all 3 retries exhausted — giving up (exitCode=1)' after 18m25s. This is the open token-budget issue #35661 — Linter Miner is a heavy linter-aggregation workflow whose effective-token consumption now reliably blows the 25M cap. FIX OPTIONS: (a) reduce Linter Miner's per-run scope / chunk its work, (b) raise the effective-token budget for this workflow if justified, (c) make the harness fail fast on a budget-429 (retrying with --continue cannot help once the cap is hit — it just burns ~92s x3). The Failure Investigator's open #35661 covers the budget class.

issues.title: NEW: golangci-lint release tarball downloaded as non-gzip body -> tar exit 2 -> agent job fails before agent runs (0 turns)
issues.detail: Run 26690499578 (PR Sous Chef, copilot/gpt-5-mini). The 'Install development dependencies' step ran 'make install-golangci-lint' which downloads golangci-lint v2.12.2 from github releases and pipes curl|tar. The release endpoint returned a non-gzip body (truncated/HTML/rate-limited); 'tar' aborted ('not in gzip format') and the step exited 2, failing the agent job at turns=0/tok=0. Transient and shared: ANY workflow using this setup step is exposed. The Failure Investigator (26692427111) independently diagnosed this and filed a durable tracking issue. FIX (from that analysis): in the install-golangci-lint Makefile target (~Makefile:403) download to a temp file, verify gzip magic bytes / HTTP 200 before extracting, retry with backoff (3x), and pin+verify a checksum; prefer Actions cache / vendored binary.

issues.title: REACTIVATED: add_comment target=* with no number fails the whole safe_outputs job (Contribution Check)
issues.detail: Run 26689948645 (Contribution Check, copilot/gpt-5-mini). Agent SUCCEEDED but the safe_outputs step FAILED: 'Message 3 (add_comment) failed: Target is "*" but no item_number/issue_number/pull_request_number specified'. On a schedule event there is no triggering issue/PR, so a target=* add_comment with no number is structurally unsatisfiable, yet the agent emitted one despite an explicit prompt guardrail (contribution-check.md lines ~184-187). Two defects: (1) Process Safe Outputs fails the ENTIRE job on one invalid item, discarding the successful create_issue; (2) create_issue with temporary_id 'aw_summary' registered 0 temp ids, leaving the chained add_labels permanently deferred. Was in 'watch' (absent in the 05-29 partial window) — REACTIVATED on this full-window recurrence. FIX: (High) make Process Safe Outputs treat an invalid item as skipped-with-warning when >=1 item succeeded; (High) validate target=* at emit time via the MCP boundary so the agent self-corrects in-loop; (Med) fix temp-id registration on create_issue success.

issues.title: Claude Code CLI step timeouts: Smoke Claude (10min) and Failure Investigator (60min) both failed on 'Execute Claude Code CLI timed out'
issues.detail: Two claude/opus runs failed at the CLI step time limit. (A) Smoke Claude 26690105542: 'timed out after 10 minutes', 61 turns, 21x 'MCP error 0: connection closed: ... client is closing: EOF' tool-result errors; this was run_attempt 2 on the now-MERGED firewall/gateway-bump PR #35973 (firewall v0.25.58 / gateway v0.3.22); awf-squid was Healthy (NOT #35780). Because the PR is merged this is a PR-branch smoke artifact — MONITOR for the MCP-EOF cluster recurring on main. (B) [aw] Failure Investigator (6h) 26692427111: 'timed out after 60 minutes', 7.8M tokens, $7.15 (most expensive run of the day). It determined 'zero failures in the 6h window' early (~19:11) yet kept running for the full hour before the CLI step timeout. EFFICIENCY/COST concern: a failure investigator that finds nothing should converge quickly, not burn 60min/$7.15 and red-fail. Action: investigate why the Failure Investigator does not early-exit on a clean window, and consider whether 60min is the intended ceiling.

issues.title: RESOLVED/NOT-RECURRING: cache-memory git setup fatal did not recur — both affected workflows green today
issues.detail: Yesterday's critical setup_cache_memory_git.sh 'fatal: not in a git directory' (exit 128) failed 2/2 cache-memory workflows. Today BOTH passed: Chaos PR Bundle Fuzzer (26694560325, success, 31 turns) and Copilot PR Prompt Pattern Analysis (26694706324, success, 8 turns). No 'non-git files present after restore' / exit-128 signatures in the window. Marking resolved-for-now; the defensive fix (verify .git via rev-parse and re-init when the restored dir has files but no valid repo) is still worthwhile to prevent a cache-state regression, but no active failures.

issues.detail: From 05-29 (run 26662945808). issues.detail: Run 26695025552 cost $5.24 on 5.80M tokens / 74 turns / 24.46M eff_tok. Up from 05-29's $3.07 but still under the 05-28 peak of $11.33. The reversal held; it is the 2nd-highest-cost workflow today behind the Failure Investigator. Keep watching for re-escalation toward the prior peak.

issues.title: Firewall healthy at 16.6% (706/4253). Blocks dominated by (unknown) SNI + Google browser telemetry; no failure-causing blocks
